file:absolute.name: file:absolute
file:absolute.description: Whether the file is regarded as absolute or relative

file:name.name: file:name
file:name.description: The file name (relative from starting directory)

file:name.ext.single.name: file:name.ext.single
file:name.ext.single.description: The file extension. If the file extension has multiple dots, then this expression strips and only returns the last part.

file:name.noext.name: file:name.noext
file:name.noext.description: The file name (relative from starting directory) without extension

file:name.noext.single.name: file:name.noext.single
file:name.noext.single.description: The file name (relative from starting directory) without extension. If the file name has multiple dots, then this expression strips and only returns the last part.

file:onlyname.noext.name: file:onlyname.noext
file:onlyname.noext.description: †he file name (without any leading paths) without extension

file:onlyname.noext.single.name: file:onlyname.noext.single
file:onlyname.noext.single.description: †he file name (without any leading paths) without extension. If the file name has multiple dots, then this expression strips and only returns the last part.

file:parent.name: file:parent
file:parent.description: The file parent directory (null if no parent directory)

abs(exp).name: abs(exp)
abs(exp).description: Converts the message body (or expression) to a long number and return the absolute value.

assert(exp,msg).name: assert(exp,msg)
assert(exp,msg).description: Evaluates the expression and throws an exception with the message if the condition is false

attachment.name.name: attachment.name
attachment.name.description: The DataHandler for the given attachment.

attachments.name: attachments
attachments.description: All the attachments as a Map<String,DataHandler.

attachmentsContentAs(name,type).name: attachmentsContentAs(name,type)
attachmentsContentAs(name,type).description: The content of the attachment, converted to the given type.

attachmentsContentAsText.name.name: attachmentsContentAsText.name
attachmentsContentAsText.name.description: The content of the attachment as text (ie String).

attachmentsHeader(key,name).name: attachmentsHeader(key,name)
attachmentsHeader(key,name).description: The attachment header with the given name.

attachmentsHeaderAs(key,name,type).name: attachmentsHeaderAs(key,name,type)
attachmentsHeaderAs(key,name,type).description: The attachment header with the given name, converted to the given type.

attachmentsSize.name: attachmentsSize
attachmentsSize.description: The number of attachments. Is 0 if there are no attachments.

average(val...).name: average(val...)
average(val...).description: Returns the average number from all the values (integral numbers only).

base64Decode(exp).name: base64Decode(exp)
base64Decode(exp).description: Base64 decodes the message body (or expression)

base64Encode(exp).name: base64Encode(exp)
base64Encode(exp).description: Base64 encodes the message body (or expression)

bean(name.method).name: bean(name.method)
bean(name.method).description: Calls a Java bean. The name of the bean can also refer to a class name using type prefix as follows `bean:type:com.foo.MyClass`. If no method name is given then Camel will automatic attempt to find the best method to use.

bodyAs(type).name: bodyAs(type)
bodyAs(type).description: Converts the message body to the given type (classname).

bodyOneLine.name: bodyOneLine
bodyOneLine.description: Converts the body to a String and removes all line-breaks, so the string is in one line.

capitalize(exp).name: capitalize(exp)
capitalize(exp).description: Capitalizes the message body/expression as a String value (upper case every words)

ceil(exp).name: ceil(exp)
ceil(exp).description: Converts the message body (or expression) to a floating number and return the ceil value (rounded up to nearest integer).

clearAttachments.name: clearAttachments
clearAttachments.description: Removes all existing attachments on the message.

collate(num).name: collate(num)
collate(num).displayName: Group Message Body into Sub Lists
collate(num).description: The collate function iterates the message body and groups the data into sub lists of specified size. This can be used with the Splitter EIP to split a message body and group/batch the split sub message into a group of N sub lists.

concat(exp,exp,separator).name: concat(exp,exp,separator)
concat(exp,exp,separator).description: Performs a string concat using two expressions (message body as default) with optional separator

contains(exp,text).name: contains(exp,text)
contains(exp,text).description: Returns true if the message body (or expression) contains part of the text (ignore case)

convertTo(exp,type).name: convertTo(exp,type)
convertTo(exp,type).description: Converts the message body (or expression) to the specified type.

date(command).name: date(command)
date(command).description: Evaluates to a java.util.Date object. Supported commands are: `now` for current timestamp, `millis` for current timestamp in millis (unix epoch), `exchangeCreated` for the timestamp when the current exchange was created, `header.xxx` to use the Long/Date object in the header with the key xxx. `variable.xxx` to use the Long/Date in the variable with the key xxx. `exchangeProperty.xxx` to use the Long/Date object in the exchange property with the key xxx. `file` for the last modified timestamp of the file (available with a File consumer). Command accepts offsets such as: `now-24h` or `header.xxx+1h` or even `now+1h30m-100`.

date-with-timezone(command:timezone:patt.name: date-with-timezone(command:timezone:pattern)
date-with-timezone(command:timezone:patt.description: Formats the date to a String using the given date pattern, and with support for timezone. Supported commands are: `now` for current timestamp, `exchangeCreated` for the timestamp when the current exchange was created, `header.xxx` to use the Long/Date object in the header with the key xxx. `variable.xxx` to use the Long/Date in the variable with the key xxx. `exchangeProperty.xxx` to use the Long/Date object in the exchange property with the key xxx. `file` for the last modified timestamp of the file (available with a File consumer). Command accepts offsets such as: `now-24h` or `header.xxx+1h` or even `now+1h30m-100`.

distinct(val...).name: distinct(val...)
distinct(val...).description: Returns a set of all the values with duplicates removed

empty(type).name: empty(type)
empty(type).description: Creates a new empty object (decided by type). Use `string` to create an empty String. Use `list` to create an empty `java.util.ArrayList`. Use `map` to create an empty `java.util.LinkedHashMap`. Use `set` to create an empty `java.util.LinkedHashSet`.

env.name.name: env.name
env.name.description: The OS environment variable with the given name

exception.name: exception
exception.description: The exception object on the exchange (also from caught exceptions), is null if no exception present.

exception.message.name: exception.message
exception.message.description: The exception message (also from caught exceptions), is null if no exception present.

exception.stackTrace.name: exception.stackTrace
exception.stackTrace.description: The exception stacktrace (also from caught exceptions), is null if no exception present.

exchangeProperty.name.name: exchangeProperty.name
exchangeProperty.name.description: The exchange property with the given name

filter(exp,fun).name: filter(exp,fun)
filter(exp,fun).description: Returns a List containing the values that satisfy the predicate function (returning true)

floor(exp).name: floor(exp)
floor(exp).description: Converts the message body (or expression) to a floating number and return the floor value (rounded down to nearest integer).

forEach(exp,fun).name: forEach(exp,fun)
forEach(exp,fun).description: Returns a List containing the values returned by the function when applied to each value from the input expression

fromRouteId.name: fromRouteId
fromRouteId.description: The original route id where this exchange was created.

function(name,exp).name: function(name,exp)
function(name,exp).description: Invokes a custom function with the given name using the message body (or expression) as input parameter.

hash(exp,algorithm).name: hash(exp,algorithm)
hash(exp,algorithm).description: Returns a hashed value (string in hex decimal) of the message body/expression using JDK MessageDigest. The algorithm can be SHA-256 (default) or SHA3-256.

header.name.name: header.name
header.name.description: The message header with the given name

headerAs(key,type).name: headerAs(key,type)
headerAs(key,type).description: Converts the message header to the given type (classname).

headers.name: headers
headers.description: Returns all the message headers in a Map

hostName.name: hostName
hostName.description: Returns the local hostname (may be empty if not possible to resolve).

iif(predicate,trueExp,falseExp).name: iif(predicate,trueExp,falseExp)
iif(predicate,trueExp,falseExp).description: Evaluates the predicate and returns the value of trueExp or falseExp. This function is similar to the ternary operator in Java.

isAlpha(exp).name: isAlpha(exp)
isAlpha(exp).description: Whether the message body (or expression) is alphabetic value (A..Z). For more advanced checks use the `regex` operator.

isAlphaNumeric(exp).name: isAlphaNumeric(exp)
isAlphaNumeric(exp).description: Whether the message body (or expression) is alphanumeric value (A..Z0-9). For more advanced checks use the `regex` operator.

isEmpty(exp).name: isEmpty(exp)
isEmpty(exp).description: Whether the message body (or expression) is null or empty (list/map types are tested if they have 0 elements).

isNumeric(exp).name: isNumeric(exp)
isNumeric(exp).description: Whether the message body (or expression) is numeric value (0..9). For more advanced checks use the `regex` operator.

join(separator,prefix,exp).name: join(separator,prefix,exp)
join(separator,prefix,exp).description: The join function iterates the message body/expression and joins the data into a string. The separator is by default a comma. The prefix is optional. The join uses the message body as source by default. It is possible to refer to another source (simple language) such as a header via the exp parameter. For example `join('&','id=','$\{header.ids}')`.

jq(input,exp).name: jq(input,exp)
jq(input,exp).description: When working with JSon data, then this allows using the JQ language, for example, to extract data from the message body (in JSon format). This requires having camel-jq JAR on the classpath. For input (optional), you can choose `header:key`, `exchangeProperty:key` or `variable:key` to use as input for the JSon payload instead of the message body.

jsonpath(input,exp).name: jsonpath(input,exp)
jsonpath(input,exp).description: When working with JSon data, then this allows using the JSonPath language, for example, to extract data from the message body (in JSon format). This requires having camel-jsonpath JAR on the classpath. For input (optional), you can choose `header:key`, `exchangeProperty:key` or `variable:key` to use as input for the JSon payload instead of the message body.

kindOfType(exp).name: kindOfType(exp)
kindOfType(exp).description: What kind of type is the value (null,number,string,boolean,array,object)

length(exp).name: length(exp)
length(exp).description: The payload length (number of bytes) of the message body (or expression).

list(val...).name: list(val...)
list(val...).description: The list function creates an ArrayList with the given set of values.

load(file).name: load(file)
load(file).description: Loads the content of the resource from classpath (cannot load from file-system to avoid dangerous situations).

mandatoryBodyAs(type).name: mandatoryBodyAs(type)
mandatoryBodyAs(type).description: Converts the message body to the given type (classname). If the body is null then the function will fail with an exception

map(key1,value1,...).name: map(key1,value1,...)
map(key1,value1,...).description: The map function creates a LinkedHashMap with the given set of pairs.

max(val...).name: max(val...)
max(val...).description: Returns the maximum number from all the values (integral numbers only).

messageAs(type).name: messageAs(type)
messageAs(type).description: Converts the message to the given type (classname).

messageHistory(boolean).name: messageHistory(boolean)
messageHistory(boolean).description: The message history of the current exchange (how it has been routed). This is similar to the route stack-trace message history the error handler logs in case of an unhandled exception. The boolean can be used to turn off detailed information to be less verbose, and avoid printing sensitive data from the message.

messageTimestamp.name: messageTimestamp
messageTimestamp.description: The message timestamp (millis since epoc) that this message originates from. Some systems like JMS, Kafka, AWS have a timestamp on the event/message that Camel received. This method returns the timestamp if a timestamp exists. The message timestamp and exchange created are different. An exchange always has a created timestamp which is the local timestamp when Camel created the exchange. The message timestamp is only available in some Camel components when the consumer is able to extract the timestamp from the source event. If the message has no timestamp, then 0 is returned.

min(val...).name: min(val...)
min(val...).description: Returns the minimum number from all the values (integral numbers only).

newEmpty(type).name: newEmpty(type)
newEmpty(type).description: Creates a new empty object (decided by type). Use `string` to create an empty String. Use `list` to create an empty `java.util.ArrayList`. Use `map` to create an empty `java.util.LinkedHashMap`. Use `set` to create an empty `java.util.LinkedHashSet`.

normalizeWhitespace(exp).name: normalizeWhitespace(exp)
normalizeWhitespace(exp).description: Normalizes the whitespace in the message body (or expression) by cleaning up excess whitespaces.

not.name: not
not.description: Evaluates the predicate and returns the opposite.

originalBody.name: originalBody
originalBody.description: The original incoming body (only available if allowUseOriginalMessage=true).

pad(exp,width,separator).name: pad(exp,width,separator)
pad(exp,width,separator).description: Pads the expression with extra padding if necessary, according the the total width. The separator is by default a space. If the width is negative then padding to the right, otherwise to the left.

pretty(exp).name: pretty(exp)
pretty(exp).description: Converts the expression to a String, and attempts to pretty print if JSon or XML, otherwise the expression is returned as the String value.

prettyBody.name: prettyBody
prettyBody.description: Converts the body to a String, and attempts to pretty print if JSon or XML; otherwise the body is returned as the String value.

properties:key:default.name: properties:key:default
properties:key:default.description: Lookup a property placeholder with the given key. If the key does not exist nor has a value, then an optional default value can be specified.

propertiesExist:key.name: propertiesExist:key
propertiesExist:key.description: Checks whether a property placeholder with the given key exists or not. The result can be negated by prefixing the key with !

quote(exp).name: quote(exp)
quote(exp).description: Returns the message body (or expression) as a double quoted string

random(min,max).name: random(min,max)
random(min,max).description: Returns a random number between min and max (exclusive)

range(min,max).name: range(min,max)
range(min,max).description: Returns a list of increasing integers between the given interval (exclusive)

ref:name.name: ref:name
ref:name.description: To look up a bean from the Registry with the given name.

replace(from,to,exp).name: replace(from,to,exp)
replace(from,to,exp).description: Replace all the string values in the message body/expression. To make it easier to replace single and double quotes, then you can use XML escaped values `\"` as double quote, `\'` as single quote, and `\∅` as empty value.

reverse(val...).name: reverse(val...)
reverse(val...).description: Returns a list of all the values, but in reverse order

routeGroup.name: routeGroup
routeGroup.description: The route group of the current route the Exchange is being routed. Not all routes have a group assigned, so this may be null.

routeId.name: routeId
routeId.description: The route id of the current route the Exchange is being routed

safeQuote(exp).name: safeQuote(exp)
safeQuote(exp).description: Returns the message body (or expression) safely quoted if needed

setHeader(name,type,exp).name: setHeader(name,type,exp)
setHeader(name,type,exp).description: Sets a message header with the given expression (optional converting to the given type)

setVariable(key,exp).name: setVariable(key,exp)
setVariable(key,exp).description: Sets an attachment with payload from the message body/expression.

setVariable(name,type,exp).name: setVariable(name,type,exp)
setVariable(name,type,exp).description: Sets a variable with the given expression (optional converting to the given type)

shuffle(val...).name: shuffle(val...)
shuffle(val...).description: Returns a list of all the values shuffled in random order

size(exp).name: size(exp)
size(exp).description: Returns the number of elements in collection or array based payloads. If the value is null then 0 is returned, otherwise 1.

skip(num).name: skip(num)
skip(num).displayName: Skip First Items from the Message Body
skip(num).description: The skip function iterates the message body and skips the first number of items. This can be used with the Splitter EIP to split a message body and skip the first N number of items.

split(exp,separator).name: split(exp,separator)
split(exp,separator).description: Splits the message body/expression as a String value using the separator into a String array

stepId.name: stepId
stepId.description: Returns the id of the current step the Exchange is being routed.

substring(head,tail).name: substring(head,tail)
substring(head,tail).description: Returns a substring of the message body/expression. If only one positive number, then the returned string is clipped from the beginning. If only one negative number, then the returned string is clipped from the beginning. Otherwise the returned string is clipped between the head and tail positions.

substringAfter(exp,before).name: substringAfter(exp,before)
substringAfter(exp,before).description: Returns a substring of the message body/expression that comes after. Returns null if nothing comes after.

substringBefore(exp,before).name: substringBefore(exp,before)
substringBefore(exp,before).description: Returns a substring of the message body/expression that comes before. Returns null if nothing comes before.

substringBetween(exp,after,before).name: substringBetween(exp,after,before)
substringBetween(exp,after,before).description: Returns a substring of the message body/expression that are between after and before. Returns null if nothing comes between.

sum(val...).name: sum(val...)
sum(val...).description: Sums together all the values as integral numbers. This function can also be used to subtract by using negative numbers.

sys.name.name: sys.name
sys.name.description: The JVM system property with the given name

threadId.name: threadId
threadId.description: Returns the id of the current thread. Can be used for logging.

threadName.name: threadName
threadName.description: Returns the name of the current thread. Can be used for logging.

throwException(type,msg).name: throwException(type,msg)
throwException(type,msg).description: Deliberately throws an error. Uses IllegalArgumentException by default if no type is specified (use fully qualified classname).

trim(exp).name: trim(exp)
trim(exp).description: The trim function trims the message body (or expression) by removing all leading and trailing white spaces.

type:name.field.name: type:name.field
type:name.field.description: To refer to a type or field by its classname. To refer to a field, you can append .FIELD_NAME. For example, you can refer to the constant field from Exchange as: `org.apache.camel.Exchange.FILE_NAME`

unquote(exp).name: unquote(exp)
unquote(exp).description: Returns the message body (or expression) with any leading/ending quotes removed

uuid(type).name: uuid(type)
uuid(type).description: Returns a UUID using the Camel `UuidGenerator`. You can choose between `default`, `classic`, `short` and `simple` as the type. If no type is given, the default is used. It is also possible to use a custom `UuidGenerator` and bind the bean to the Registry with an id. For example `${uuid(myGenerator)}` where the ID is _myGenerator_.

variable.name.name: variable.name
variable.name.description: The variable with the given name

variableAs(key,type).name: variableAs(key,type)
variableAs(key,type).description: Converts the variable to the given type (classname).

variables.name: variables
variables.description: Returns all the variables from the current Exchange in a Map

xpath(input,exp).name: xpath(input,exp)
xpath(input,exp).description: When working with XML data, then this allows using the XPath language, for example, to extract data from the message body (in XML format). This requires having camel-xpath JAR on the classpath. For input (optional), you can choose `header:key`, `exchangeProperty:key` or `variable:key` to use as input for the XML payload instead of the message body.
